title: PGOB engine-agnostic comparison (FlyGym v2, 48-d gains) — NOT adversarial; two interchangeable back-ends of ONE behaviour-only formulation
framing: SNPE is a PGOB engine, not a competitor. Both engines consume the same behaviour observables; we characterise their complementary trade-offs.

optimizer_engine_SPSA.note: behaviour-matched; lands on behaviourally-equivalent manifold (params drift 0.136->0.161 while behaviour fits)
optimizer_engine_SPSA.behaviour_recovery: 94.9% of random->expert aggregate-W1 gap (13.30->10.78 vs expert 10.64)

takeaway: Both PGOB engines solve the behaviour-only inverse problem on the same task. The amortised neural-posterior engine additionally pins parameters tighter (rel 0.018) and returns a calibrated posterior; the black-box optimiser gives a fast per-target point estimate that settles on the behaviour manifold. Engine-agnostic by construction.
